quote.text: The difference between theory and practice is that in theory, there is no difference between theory and practice
quote.author: Richard Moore

links.title: Tailwind CSS v2.0
links.description: Tailwind has definitely been the CSS framework of the year. This new release (Tailwind CSS v2.0) comes with some new interesting features, including an all-new colour palette, dark mode support, and tons more!

links.title: 7 Git tricks that changed my life
links.description: Some helpful Git tips that might change the way you work with the popular version control system.

links.title: 10 Insights from Adopting TypeScript at Scale
links.description: Rob Palmer shares some of the insights & lessons learned during Bloomberg Engineering's journey to adopt TypeScript as a first-class supported language.

links.title: The Math of Password Hashing Algorithms and Entropy
links.description: Long passwords and one-way password hashing are standard strategies used to increase security and protect your user's passwords. Let's take a look at how they work and some new ideas to improve it.

links.title: EOS free & open source icons
links.description: Handcrafted iconic font designed using the Material Design guidelines.

links.title: Using Vue as a Node.js Static Site Generator
links.description: Here's how you can build a static site blog using vanilla Vue and Node.js.

links.title: The Case Against Anti-Flicker Snippets
links.description: Measuring the impact of AB testing tools anti-flicker snippets and why I think they're a symptom of a bigger problem

book.title: Elixir in Action
book.author: Saša Jurić
book.description: Elixir in Action teaches you to apply the new Elixir programming language to practical problems associated with scalability, concurrency, fault tolerance, and high availability. Elixir is a modern programming language that takes advantage of BEAM, the Erlang virtual machine, without the burden of Erlang's complex syntax and conventions. Elixir gives you Ruby-like elegance with the power to develop bulletproof distributed server systems that can handle massive numbers of simultaneous clients and run with almost no downtime.
